external_channels.note: No external delivery channel exists in the schema or code; the tqe-in-app-alert/v1 definition has no channel field and any extra field is rejected fail-closed at validation.

implementation_slices.1_schema_validation: src/tw_quant_engine/alerts.py validate_alert() — tqe-in-app-alert/v1 fail-closed rejection (bad schema, unknown condition/indicator/field, target outside admitted universe, malformed dedup/expiry, unknown fields incl. any channel field).
implementation_slices.2_evaluator: src/tw_quant_engine/alerts.py evaluate_alerts() — deterministic, explicit-now, over admitted read-model data (price_threshold close, indicator_threshold ma/ema/rsi/macd/kd/atr from the kline read model); src/tw_quant_engine/desktop_sidecar.py GET /alerts loopback route.
implementation_slices.3_dedup_expiry: src/tw_quant_engine/alerts.py — once_per_session / cooldown_seconds via caller-owned session_state; session / until expiry (until boundary inclusive-expired at now >= until).
implementation_slices.4_persistence: serialize_alert_store()/parse_alert_store() flat versioned JSON tqe-in-app-alerts/v1; session-expiry definitions persist within the session and are dropped at load when a new session starts (sessionStorage session marker in ui/dashboard/app.js; a desktop app launch is a new session); desktop Tauri commands frontend/src-tauri/src/alerts.rs load_alerts/save_alerts (atomic write, watchlist.rs style) registered in frontend/src-tauri/src/lib.rs; ui/dashboard/app.js dual path — Tauri invoke when the desktop shell is present, localStorage key tqe-in-app-alerts.v1 otherwise.
implementation_slices.5_presentation: ui/dashboard/app.js alertsMarkup() research-only alerts panel in the market section; reducer/actions in ui/dashboard/dashboard-core.js; no order-like affordance, wording, or control.
implementation_slices.6_fixture_tests: tests/fixtures/p6/alerts.json + tests/test_p6_in_app_alerts.py (14 tests: exact fired set, skip reasons, determinism, dedup replay, expiry boundary, fail-closed store parse, in-app-only delivery stub, sidecar route); tests/dashboard-core.test.cjs extended (alert reducer, store payload, in_app-only event merge).

acceptance_notes: Done: all seven implementation slices are implemented and verified offline per the work-unit verification commands; p4_research_closure.py passed before and after implementation.
Done: evaluation runs only in the local engine (src/tw_quant_engine/alerts.py) over admitted fixture/read-model data; the only delivery is the in-app event (channel in_app, research_only true).

Done: alert definitions persist session-locally as flat versioned JSON (tqe-in-app-alerts/v1) and never leave the local machine; firing history (session_state) is session-local in-memory and is not persisted.

Resolved (2026-07-22, user-approved extension): the earlier deviation is closed — the desktop Tauri command surface now includes alerts::load_alerts/save_alerts (frontend/src-tauri/src/alerts.rs, atomic write following watchlist.rs, same app-data directory, validated tqe-in-app-alerts/v1 store). The user explicitly approved this write-scope expansion on 2026-07-22; scripts/p4_research_closure.py now enforces it via the renamed check tauri_write_scope_is_watchlist_and_alerts (still excluding any order surface). cargo check and cargo test --lib pass; ui/dashboard/app.js uses the Tauri path when the desktop shell is present and the identical localStorage format otherwise.

Verified in a real browser (scripts/dashboard-alerts-smoke.cjs, headless Chromium): panel renders, research-only wording present, no order-like affordance in the DOM, alert creation persists, evaluation renders in-app events via loopback only (external_requests []), until-expiry alerts survive reload, invalid definitions are rejected with HTTP 400.

Not done: human acceptance (this status stays implemented_pending_human_acceptance until the gate owner accepts); no external delivery channel; no provider calls; StrategySpec remains not_admitted; capabilities 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 remain untouched.
The alerts panel degrades to inert when the loopback sidecar is unreachable (research-only error state, no firing).
2026-07-22 human acceptance: checks 1-4 and 6 pass. Check 5 (persistence & expiry) failed on first test — a session-expiry alert did not disappear after closing and reopening the browser tab.

Root cause of the check-5 failure (fixed 2026-07-22): the alert store had no session concept. Session-expiry definitions were dropped at save time (so a reload within the same session could not keep them) and the loader never pruned them (so any session-expiry definition present in the store survived across sessions).

Fix: the store now keeps session-expiry definitions so F5 in the same tab keeps them; on load the app tracks the page session with a sessionStorage marker and, on a new session (new browser tab or desktop app launch), drops session-expiry definitions and persists the pruned store. Touched: ui/dashboard/dashboard-core.js (alertStorePayload, dropSessionAlertDefinitions), ui/dashboard/app.js (beginAlertsSession, loadAlertDefinitions in ensureAlertsRuntime), src/tw_quant_engine/alerts.py (serialize_alert_store), tests/dashboard-core.test.cjs, tests/test_p6_in_app_alerts.py, scripts/dashboard-alerts-smoke.cjs.

Re-verified offline after the fix (2026-07-22T15:27Z): node tests/dashboard-core.test.cjs pass; python3 -B -m unittest discover -s tests 152 tests OK (1 skipped); node scripts/dashboard-alerts-smoke.cjs 13/13 including new_session_drops_session_alerts (a fresh tab keeps only the until-expiry alert and the persisted store is pruned; F5 in the same tab keeps both); python3 scripts/p4_research_closure.py pass.
Check 5 awaits the gate owner's re-test; status stays implemented_pending_human_acceptance until then.
